# Vizzy

> Vizzy is a TypeScript library for interactive visuals that live inside a webpage: function graphs, geometric diagrams, algorithm walkthroughs, explorable essays. Browser-native, declarative, interaction-first. Not a charting library, not a research notebook, not a Python-to-video pipeline.

This file bundles the full public docs, README highlights, and common recipes into a single document for LLM ingestion. Canonical sources live at https://vizzyjs.dev and https://github.com/blparker/vizzy.

- npm: `@vizzyjs/core`, `@vizzyjs/renderer-canvas`, `@vizzyjs/react`
- Status: pre-1.0 (`0.1.x`); API stabilizing, may move between minor versions.
- License: MIT.

---

## Install

```bash
npm install @vizzyjs/core @vizzyjs/renderer-canvas
# or: pnpm add / yarn add
```

React users also install `@vizzyjs/react`. KaTeX is an optional peer dep of `@vizzyjs/renderer-canvas`; install it if you use `tex()`.

---

## The shortest runnable example

```typescript
import { circle, fadeIn, sky } from '@vizzyjs/core';
import { createScene } from '@vizzyjs/renderer-canvas';

const canvas = document.querySelector<HTMLCanvasElement>('canvas')!;
const { add, play, grid } = createScene(canvas);

grid();
const c = circle({ radius: 1, color: sky });
add(c);
await play(fadeIn(c));
```

---

## Scene setup

`createScene(canvas)` returns a `BoundScene` you destructure. Commonly used properties:

| Property | Purpose |
| --- | --- |
| `add(shape)` | Add a shape to the scene (auto-renders) |
| `grid()` | Add a coordinate grid |
| `play(animation, ...)` | Run animations (returns Promise) |
| `wait(seconds)` | Pause |
| `controls` | HTML controls (sliders, checkboxes, color pickers, selects, text) |
| `interact` | Draggable / hoverable / clickable on shapes |

Callback form:

```typescript
import { renderScene } from '@vizzyjs/renderer-canvas';

renderScene(canvas, ({ add }) => {
    add(circle({ color: sky }));
});
```

React:

```tsx
import { useScene } from '@vizzyjs/react';
import { circle, fadeIn, sky } from '@vizzyjs/core';

export function Demo() {
    const ref = useScene(({ add, play, grid }) => {
        grid();
        const c = circle({ color: sky });
        add(c);
        play(fadeIn(c));
    });
    return <canvas ref={ref} width={800} height={457} />;
}
```

`useScene` defaults `autoResize: true`.

---

## Coordinate system

14x8 world units. Origin at center. Y points up.

```
         (0, 4)
           |
(-7, 0) ---+--- (7, 0)
           |
         (0, -4)
```

You never deal with pixels. DPR scaling and canvas resize are handled for you.

---

## Shapes

Factory functions. Every shape starts at the origin. Use `.shift(dx, dy)`, `.moveTo([x, y])`, or `.nextTo(shape, direction)` to position.

### Basic shapes

```typescript
grid();
add(
    circle({ color: sky }),
    rect({ width: 2, height: 1.2, color: emerald }).shift(3, 0),
    triangle({ color: red }).shift(-3, 0),
);
```

### Full list of factories

`circle`, `rect`, `line`, `arrow`, `polygon`, `regularPolygon`, `triangle`, `square`, `ellipse`, `arc`, `star`, `point`, `text`, `tex`, `dashedLine`, `vector`, `doubleArrow`, `curvedArrow`, `arcBetweenPoints`, `rightAngle`, `surroundingRectangle`, `numberLine`, `axes`, `functionGraph`, `brace`, `braceOver`, `braceBetween`, `angleShape`, `edgeLabel`, `lineLabel`, `label`, `group`.

### Direction constants

`UP`, `DOWN`, `LEFT`, `RIGHT`, `UP_LEFT`, `UP_RIGHT`, `DOWN_LEFT`, `DOWN_RIGHT`, `ORIGIN`.

### Colors

Full Tailwind palette: `red`, `orange`, `amber`, `yellow`, `lime`, `green`, `emerald`, `teal`, `cyan`, `sky`, `blue`, `indigo`, `violet`, `purple`, `fuchsia`, `pink`, `rose`, `slate`, `gray`, `zinc`, `neutral`, `stone`, plus `white` and `black`. Each color has shades `[50]` through `[950]` and an `.alpha(n)` method.

```typescript
add(
    circle({ color: sky }),                                          // shorthand
    circle({ style: { stroke: sky, fill: sky[900] } }),              // full style
    circle({ style: { stroke: violet, fill: violet.alpha(0.3) } }),  // alpha
    circle({ style: { fill: orange, stroke: null } }),               // no stroke
);
```

### Groups

`group(...shapes)` composes shapes into a unit. `arrange(direction, spacing)` auto-layouts children.

```typescript
const row = group(
    circle({ radius: 0.4, color: violet }),
    circle({ radius: 0.4, color: pink }),
    circle({ radius: 0.4, color: orange }),
);
row.arrange(RIGHT, 0.5);
row.shift(-3, 0);
add(row);
```

`moveToFront(shape)` and `moveToBack(shape)` set z-ordering within a group.

### Text and TeX

`text({ content, position, style })` renders plain text. `label(shape, content, direction)` positions text near a shape. `tex({ content, ... })` renders LaTeX via KaTeX (peer dep).

### Axes and function graphs

```typescript
const ax = axes({
    xRange: [-5, 5, 1],
    yRange: [-3, 3, 1],
    includeNumbers: true,
    color: neutral[400],
});
add(ax);

add(functionGraph({
    fn: (x) => Math.sin(x),
    axes: ax,
    style: { stroke: sky, strokeWidth: 0.05 },
}));
```

Shorthand: `ax.plot(fn, opts)` creates and adds in one call. `c2p([x, y])` and `p2c([x, y])` convert between axis coords and world coords. Function graphs handle discontinuities without phantom vertical lines.

---

## Animations

Async/await, no schedulers.

```typescript
await play(fadeIn(c));                // one at a time
await play(fadeIn(a), fadeIn(b));     // simultaneous
await wait(0.5);
```

A shape passed to `fadeIn` / `create` is auto-added to the scene. Don't call `add()` first if you want the animation to introduce it.

### Built-in animations

- `fadeIn(shape)`, `fadeOut(shape)`
- `create(shape)`: progressively draw the stroke
- `animateShift(shape, [dx, dy])`, `animateMoveTo(shape, [x, y])`
- `animateRotate(shape, radians)`, `animateScale(shape, factor)`
- `animateColor(shape, { stroke?, fill? })`, `animateOpacity(shape, target)`
- `during(callback)`: runs every frame while other animations play; receives eased `t` from 0 to 1. Useful for dynamic labels and computed positions.

### Options

```typescript
await play(animateShift(c, [3, 0]), { duration: 2 });
await play(animateShift(c, [-3, 0]), { duration: 0.5, easing: easeInOutCubic });
```

Easings: `linear`, `easeInQuad`/`Cubic`/`Sine`, `easeOutQuad`/`Cubic`/`Sine`, `easeInOutQuad`/`Cubic`/`Sine`, `smooth` (default, Hermite smoothstep), `smoother` (Perlin smootherstep).

---

## Interactivity

### Controls (HTML overlay)

```typescript
controls.panel();
const radius = controls.slider('Radius', { min: 0.2, max: 3, value: 1, step: 0.1 });
const c = circle({ color: sky });
add(c);
controls.onUpdate(() => { c.radius = radius.value; });
```

| Control | Signature | Returns |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Slider | `controls.slider(label, { min, max, value, step })` | `ControlHandle<number>` |
| Checkbox | `controls.checkbox(label, { value })` | `ControlHandle<boolean>` |
| Color | `controls.color(label, { value })` | `ControlHandle<string>` |
| Select | `controls.select(label, { options, value })` | `ControlHandle<string>` |
| Text | `controls.text(label, { value, placeholder })` | `ControlHandle<string>` |

Each handle: `.value`, `.set(v)`, `.onChange(fn)`.

### Mouse interaction on shapes

```typescript
interact.draggable(dot, {
    onDrag(pos) {
        dot.moveTo(pos);
    },
    constrainX: [-4, 4],
    constrainY: [0, 0],
});

interact.hoverable(r, {
    onEnter() { r.setStyle({ fill: emerald[900] }); },
    onLeave() { r.setStyle({ fill: null }); },
});

interact.clickable(btn, { onClick() { /* ... */ } });
```

Each returns an unsubscribe function: `const unsub = interact.draggable(shape, opts); unsub();`. Cursors are set automatically (`grab`/`grabbing` for draggable, `pointer` for hoverable and clickable).

---

## Architecture (for contributors)

- **Retained scene graph.** `add(shape)` stages the shape; the scene re-renders on next frame.
- **Visitor pattern.** `Renderer` in `@vizzyjs/core` is backend-agnostic. `@vizzyjs/renderer-canvas` implements Canvas2D. SVG is next.
- **Shallow hierarchy.** Most shapes extend `Shape` or `PathShape`. Composites extend `Group`.
- **Factories, not classes.** Public API is factory functions; classes are internal.

### Repository layout

- `packages/core` — shapes, scene graph, animations, math, style
- `packages/renderer-canvas` — Canvas2D renderer, `createScene`, controls, interaction
- `packages/react` — `useScene` hook
- `packages/docs` — VitePress docs (vizzyjs.dev)
- `packages/playground` — local sandbox with Monaco editor
- `apps/hub` — Next.js app for hub.vizzyjs.dev

### Commands

Node 22+, pnpm 10+.

- `pnpm install`
- `pnpm build`
- `pnpm test` (vitest)
- `pnpm typecheck`
- `pnpm lint`
- `pnpm docs` / `pnpm playground` / `pnpm hub`

### Conventions

- 4-space indentation, single quotes, TypeScript strict mode.
- ESM bundler resolution, no `.js` extensions on local imports.
- Factories are public; classes are internal.
- No em-dashes in prose (commits, docs, comments).
- Default to no comments; only when WHY is non-obvious.

### Adding a shape

New file in `packages/core/src/shapes/`. Add a factory in `factories.ts`. Export both from `packages/core/src/shapes/index.ts`. If it benefits from a docs snippet, add it under `packages/docs/snippets/`.

### Adding an animation

New file in `packages/core/src/animation/`. Follow `fade.ts` or `transform.ts`. Use `makeAnimation()` to cut boilerplate. Return an `Animation` with `begin`, `update`, `finish` hooks.

---

## Gotchas

- Shapes added with `add()` are visible immediately. For entrance animations, skip `add()` and let `play(fadeIn(shape))` add the shape for you.
- Use world units, not pixels. `radius: 1` is a unit circle.
- Don't import from package internals. Only the top-level entry is public.
- Don't instantiate shape classes directly. Use factories: `circle(...)`, not `new Circle(...)`.
- Vizzy is its own library, shaped around the browser, async/await, and live interaction. It is not a manim port and should not be positioned as one.

---

## Roadmap

- SVG renderer (`Renderer` interface is already backend-agnostic).
- Richer interaction primitives (constrained dragging, keyboard handlers, animated drag paths).
- Community shape registry (shadcn-style: copy shapes into your project).
- 1.0 API freeze.
- Scene export to GIF / MP4 from code.

Not on the roadmap for 1.0: 3D rendering, a chart DSL, Svelte / Vue bindings (welcome as community packages after 1.0).

---

## Hub (hub.vizzyjs.dev)

In-browser playground. Publish and share vizzes. Every published viz gets a `/v/[id]` viewer page, a `/v/[id]/edit` editor, and an embeddable `/embed/[id]` iframe suitable for blog posts and docs.

---

- Docs: https://vizzyjs.dev
- GitHub: https://github.com/blparker/vizzy
- Hub: https://hub.vizzyjs.dev

- Discussions: https://github.com/blparker/vizzy/discussions
